All Security Articles & Guides

Breaking CVE analysis, security tool deep-dives, and practical penetration testing guides. Updated as security news breaks.

Cisco SD-WAN CVE-2026-20127: CVSS 10.0 Auth Bypass Under Active Exploitation — Patch by Tomorrow

Published: February 26, 2026

A CVSS 10.0 authentication bypass in Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN vSmart and vManage is being actively exploited by nation-state threat actor UAT-8616. CISA Emergency Directive 26-03 mandates federal agencies patch by February 27, 2026.

Read Article →

How China's UNC2814 Used Google Sheets as a Hacking Command Centre — And Got Caught

Published: February 26, 2026

Chinese APT UNC2814's GRIDTIDE campaign used Google Sheets cells as a command-and-control channel, compromising 53 organisations in 42 countries before Google disrupted the infrastructure on February 25, 2026.

Read Article →

Zyxel Routers CVE-2025-13942: Unauthenticated RCE Affects 120,000 Exposed Devices — Patch Now

Published: February 26, 2026

Critical command injection vulnerabilities in 12+ Zyxel router models expose 120,000 internet-facing devices to unauthenticated RCE via UPnP SOAP requests. Security updates released February 25, 2026. Common in Irish and UK ISP-distributed hardware.

Read Article →

CVE-2026-21531: Azure SDK for Python RCE — Deserialization Bug Exposes Cloud Applications

Published: February 24, 2026

Critical CVSS 9.8 deserialization vulnerability in Azure AI Language Python SDK allows unauthenticated remote code execution. Any app using azure-ai-language-conversations 1.0.0-beta is at risk.

Read Article →

Model Distillation Attacks: How DeepSeek and Chinese AI Firms Extracted Claude at Industrial Scale

Published: February 24, 2026

Anthropic has exposed three Chinese AI companies — DeepSeek, Moonshot AI, and MiniMax — for running industrial-scale model distillation campaigns via 16 million fraudulent Claude API queries. Here's how the attacks worked, how they were detected, and what it means for AI API security.

Read Article →

Claude Code Security vs. Active Penetration Testing: The AI Arms Race Has Reached Your Codebase

Published: February 23, 2026

Anthropic launched Claude Code Security on February 21, 2026. AI code scanning is now real. Here's what it catches, where it stops, and why your security team still needs active penetration testing.

Read Article →

Burp Suite Costs $449/yr Per User. Here's What a 5-Person Team Actually Spends.

Published: February 22, 2026

Burp Suite Pro is $449/user/yr. Enterprise starts at $3,999/yr. Here's the real total cost for security teams in 2026 — and what Burp doesn't cover.

Everything security teams need to know about AI-powered and automated pentesting in 2026: how it works, what it covers, what to look for in a platform, and how to get started.

Why Your Security Scanner Isn't a Penetration Test

Published: February 21, 2026

Vulnerability scanners find known CVEs. Penetration tests find what attackers actually exploit. Here's the critical difference — and what you're missing if you rely on scans alone.

Read Article →

CVE-2026-22769: Dell RecoverPoint CVSS 10.0 Zero-Day Exploited by China-Nexus Hackers Since 2024

Published: February 20, 2026

CISA adds CVE-2026-22769 to KEV catalog with 3-day patch deadline. Dell RecoverPoint hardcoded credential zero-day exploited by UNC6201 (Silk Typhoon) since mid-2024 — deploying BRICKSTORM, GRIMBOLT backdoors and Ghost NICs for stealthy VMware pivoting.

Read Article →

Critical VS Code Extension Vulnerabilities: 125 Million Installs At Risk (CVE-2025-65717, CVE-2025-65716, CVE-2025-65715)

Published: February 19, 2026

Four critical vulnerabilities in popular VS Code extensions — Live Server, Markdown Preview Enhanced, Code Runner, and Microsoft Live Preview — put 125 million developers at risk of file exfiltration and remote code execution. Three remain unpatched.

Critical CVE-2026-21513 MSHTML Framework zero-day vulnerability exploited in the wild. CVSS 8.8 security feature bypass enables attackers to evade Mark-of-the-Web protections.

BeyondTrust Pre-Auth RCE (CVE-2026-1731): WebSocket OS Command Injection Hits 8,500+ Vulnerable Instances

Published: February 23, 2026

CVSS 9.9 pre-authentication RCE in BeyondTrust Remote Support and Privileged Remote Access enables OS command injection via unauthenticated WebSocket. 8,500+ vulnerable instances globally — patch or restrict now.

Read Article →

Firefox SpiderMonkey WebAssembly GC RCE: One Typo, Full Renderer Compromise

Published: February 23, 2026

A single & vs | typo in Firefox SpiderMonkey's Wasm GC engine causes a type confusion leading to RCE in the renderer process — affecting 200M+ users via any malicious webpage.

Read Article →

CVE-2026-2473: GCP Vertex AI Bucket Squatting Enables Cross-Tenant RCE and Model Theft (CVSS 9.8)

Published: February 23, 2026

Google patched a critical Vertex AI vulnerability where predictable Cloud Storage bucket names allowed unauthenticated attackers to steal AI models, poison training pipelines, and execute code across tenant boundaries.

Read Article →

Critical n8n RCE Vulnerability (CVE-2026-21858): 100,000+ Servers at Risk

Published: February 23, 2026

CVSS 10.0 vulnerability in n8n workflow automation enables unauthenticated remote code execution. Learn about CVE-2026-21858, affected versions, exploitation methods, and remediation steps.

RoguePilot: How a Hidden GitHub Copilot Bug Silently Steals Your Entire Repository

Published: February 23, 2026

Orca Security discloses passive prompt injection in GitHub Copilot that leaks GITHUB_TOKEN from Codespaces — no user interaction required beyond opening a Codespace from a malicious issue.

Read Article →

About This Site

BugHunterTools publishes independent security research, CVE analysis, and practitioner guides for bug bounty hunters and penetration testers. Written for technical readers — no hype, real data, honest assessments.

Content is sourced from SecurityClaw (ClawWorks' AI-assisted security research platform) and independent analysis.

Also see: SecurityClaw Live Demos — real tool runs, actual output data.
